expectations.text: Response provides color tokens for all three traditions
expectations.evidence: Provides colors but WRONG values: Judaism=bg-blue-800 (ARE uses bg-blue-950), Christianity=bg-purple-700 (ARE uses violet, not purple), Islam=bg-green-700 (ARE uses emerald). Also includes Buddhism/Hinduism/Sikhism which are out of ARE scope.

expectations.text: Response describes the three-panel layout structure
expectations.evidence: Describes side-by-side columnar layout with Header, Reference, Text Body structure. Functionally similar to three-panel layout, though doesn't use ARE terminology.

expectations.text: Response mentions displaying the bridging note below the panels
expectations.evidence: No mention of a 'bridging note' or 'What Connects These?' section anywhere in the response. The ARE-specific concept is completely absent.

expectations.text: Response mentions using staticText for instant rendering with optional live API fetch
expectations.evidence: No mention of staticText, pre-seeded data, or the live API fetch pattern. Without-skill describes a generic layout without ARE data architecture.

needs_review: Christianity color: without-skill used 'purple' (bg-purple-700) not 'violet' (bg-violet-950). In CSS, purple and violet are distinct hues. Using bg-purple-* instead of bg-violet-* is a subtle but real visual difference.
